---
title: "Path to Systems"
subtitle: "The mathematics behind performance, concurrency, and reliability at scale"
---

## What you can do at the end of this path

- Reason about queueing, latency, and throughput from first principles
  (Little's Law, M/M/1 queues, tail latency).
- Read papers on distributed consensus, formal methods (TLA+), and
  performance modelling.
- Build mental models for cache hierarchies, branch prediction, and bit-level
  representations.

## The path

Systems leans on probability, combinatorics, discrete math, and increasingly
on formal logic and queueing theory. Bit-level reasoning starts in Tier 1 with
place value.

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
    A[Tier 1<br/>Place value, decimals] --> B[Tier 2<br/>Exponents, scientific notation]
    B --> C[Tier 3<br/>Logs, combinatorics]
    C --> D[Tier 5<br/>Discrete math, probability & statistics]
    D --> E[Tier 6<br/>Graph theory, info theory, numerical methods]
    E --> F[Tier 7 — Systems track:<br/>Queueing theory<br/>Performance modelling<br/>Formal methods]

    classDef built fill:#c8e6c9,stroke:#2e7d32
    class A built
```

## Where to start right now

The systems path begins with **Place Value** (binary, octal, hex are all
place value in different bases) in Tier 1.

## What's built / what's planned

- ✅ Tier 1: *What is a Number?* (exemplar lesson)
- 📋 Everything beyond — planned, not yet authored.
